previous_notes.text: Patient presented with elevated PSA of 17.4 ng/mL in February 2023. Digital rectal examination was unremarkable. Multiparametric MRI was arranged, showing a PI-RADS 5 lesion in the right peripheral zone. Transperineal biopsy was performed, revealing Gleason 4+4 (ISUP grade group 4) adenocarcinoma with cribriform pattern and perineural invasion. The patient was referred to urology for further management discussion. At that time, he opted for active surveillance after reviewing the risks and benefits of immediate intervention, citing his age and comorbidities. Follow-up PSA was scheduled for six months later.
previous_notes.author: Dr. H. Martens (GP)

pathology_report:
Biopsy specimen, 19 Feb 2023:
Histological examination reveals adenocarcinoma of the prostate. The Gleason score is 4+4, corresponding to ISUP grade group 4. The tumour exhibits a predominant cribriform growth pattern. Perineural invasion is present. The tumour is located in the seminal vesicles, right.

Summary of trajectory:
The histological findings across the single documented biopsy timepoint demonstrate high-grade disease with cribriform architecture and perineural invasion, consistent with aggressive biological behaviour.

AI-predicted ISUP grade group (digital pathology): grade group 5.
radiology_report:
Spaarne Gasthuis (Haarlem)
Radiology Dept
Date: 20 Jan 2025
Exam: mpMRI prostate

Clinical indication: Rising PSA (187.0 ng/mL), prior biopsy Gleason 4+4, evaluate for local extent and suspicious lesions.

FINDINGS:
Prostate volume: 57.28 mL.
T2-weighted imaging demonstrates a focal, ill-defined hypointense lesion in the right peripheral zone, extending towards the right seminal vesicle. On diffusion-weighted imaging, the lesion shows marked restricted diffusion with high signal intensity on b=1400 images and corresponding low ADC values. Dynamic contrast-enhanced sequences reveal early, intense enhancement of the lesion relative to the surrounding glandular tissue. The right seminal vesicle shows asymmetrical enlargement and signal alteration consistent with tumour infiltration. No suspicious lymphadenopathy or bone lesions are identified within the field of view.

IMPRESSION:
PI-RADS 5 lesion in the right peripheral zone with seminal vesicle involvement.
Prostate volume 57.28 mL; PSA density 3.26 ng/mL/mL.
Clinical stage cT1c.
csPCa predicted probability 0.964853.
